capabilities: Cloud migration and application modernization (AWS GovCloud, Azure Government)
DevSecOps pipeline design and CI/CD automation
FedRAMP Moderate authorization support and ATO preparation
Legacy system integration and data migration

lessonsLearned.note: Having a warm intro to the Program Director through our incumbent sub made a measurable difference. Buyers cited our understanding of their environment as a key differentiator over lower-priced competitors.
Our phased migration narrative — with explicit risk gates and rollback plans — directly addressed the buyer's fear of service disruption. Made this the centerpiece of the technical volume.
We priced 8% above the lowest offer but won on best value. The evaluators documented that our risk mitigation approach justified the premium. Don't race to the bottom on complex modernization work.

notes: Competed as SDVOSB prime with two subs. Won at Gate 3 after a very competitive BAFO round. Program Director later told us our ATO roadmap was the single most cited differentiator in the source selection decision. Contract ran 3 years, earned Exceptional CPARS across all categories.

capabilities: Cloud migration and application modernization (AWS GovCloud, Azure Government)
Data engineering and analytics (Power BI, Databricks)
FedRAMP Moderate authorization support and ATO preparation

lessonsLearned.note: The winning team had a directly relevant CMS CPARS citation at similar scale. Our references were strong but none were CMS-specific. For high-stakes CMS bids, we need a named CMS sub or prime partner with a recent citation.
We had no relationship with the CO or COR going into proposal. Made cold outreach too late — only 2 weeks before RFP drop. Earlier BD investment is critical for CMS opportunities.

Our data architecture section was undercooked. We described tools but didn't quantify outcomes or show a migration sequence. Evaluators noted 'insufficient detail on data governance and lineage tracking.'

notes: Lost in best-value evaluation. Came in second on technical, third on past performance volume. The winning firm had an incumbent-adjacent relationship and a CMS-specific CPARS. We were competitive on price. Key lesson: CMS procurement offices weigh agency-specific past performance very heavily — we need to build that reference base before pursuing another CMS replatform at this scale.

capabilities: Cloud migration and application modernization (AWS GovCloud, Azure Government)
DevSecOps pipeline design and CI/CD automation

lessonsLearned.note: PWS required CMMC Level 2 certification and at least 5 cleared personnel at TS/SCI. We had neither at the time. Pursuing without closing this gap would have been a proposal-stage disqualifier.

The draft-to-proposal window was 5 weeks. Even if we had the clearances, our proposal team was already committed to two other live proposals. Bandwidth is a real constraint — track pipeline load before committing.

notes: Made a deliberate no-bid decision at Gate 2. Capability gaps (CMMC, cleared staff) and proposal bandwidth were the deciding factors. Documented this as a strategic marker — if we invest in CMMC Level 2 certification over the next 12 months, a future DISA recompete would be a strong fit given our DevSecOps and cloud modernization track record.
